title: Plant Stand or Beverage Bucket Stand
summary: A simple four-legged 2x4 stand sized to cradle a round bucket — a planter or a galvanized beverage tub — held up by two cross pieces instead of a solid platform.
description:
Four 2x4 legs meet at a low X-shaped cross brace made from a long piece and two shorter pieces — this open cross, not a solid platform, is what holds a round bucket at a stable height while leaving the underside completely open. That open design is also what keeps this build to just seven pieces total.

**This stand is sized around a 24" round galvanized bucket** — the plan is explicit that it can be adjusted for a different size, but doesn't recommend going any larger, since a bigger bucket changes the load and balance point beyond what the leg spread is designed for. Assemble with pocket holes for clean, strong joints at the leg-to-cross connections.

Use the finished stand for a large potted plant, or fill the bucket with ice for a beverage tub at a party — the same simple frame works for both.

materials.name: Pocket hole screws, 2-1/2", or self-tapping deck screws, 3"

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut four legs at 27", one long cross piece at 24" and two short cross pieces at 11-1/4" from the 2x4, 8 ft or stud length. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to two 8 ft 2x4, 8 ft or stud length. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the cross brace
steps.body: Join the long cross piece and two short cross pieces into an X-shaped brace.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 2-1/2", or self-tapping deck screws, 3"

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the four legs to the cross brace, spread evenly for a stable base.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 2-1/2", or self-tapping deck screws, 3"

steps.title: Set the bucket
steps.body: Set the 24" round bucket into the finished stand.

unresolvedImages.alt: Simple 2x4 plant stand or beverage bucket stand
